## [Note]

- dbbot v0.8.0 promotes the MySQL post-deploy and post-uninstall acceptance checks introduced during the beta cycle to the official release line.
- The release focuses on MySQL deployment reliability, Router usability, managed release packaging, and documentation alignment for the bundled portable Ansible runtime.

## [Add]

- Add shared MySQL post-check profiles for standalone instances, asynchronous replication, MGR, InnoDB Cluster, MySQL Router, Router HA, exporters, MHA, MHA-Go, and unsafe uninstall cleanup.
- Add `router_unsafe_uninstall.yml` with confirmation, Router cleanup, optional keepalived cleanup, and post-uninstall validation.
- Add configurable package download timeouts for MySQL Server, MySQL Shell, and MySQL Router package preparation.
- Add MySQL MGR documentation in both English and Chinese.

## [Change]

- Default MySQL Router deployment to single-node App mode and require explicit `router_enable_ha: true` when keepalived VIP mode is needed.
- Improve MySQL Router package selection so the glibc package tag can be resolved automatically for MySQL Router 9.7, 8.4, and supported EL-style distributions.
- Harden service and package checks by replacing several shell probes with Ansible facts or assertions.
- Update MySQL documentation to explain automatic acceptance checks, non-interactive confirmation variables, and explicit portable Ansible paths for scripts, CI jobs, and SSH one-liners.

## [Fix]

- Fix the InnoDB Cluster completion summary so the displayed `mysqlsh` status command includes a target URI.
- Fix MySQL uninstall validation wording so it reflects the actual data, log, and runtime directories that are removed.
- Fix Router HA validation so VIP reachability and local VIP placement are checked separately.
- Fix lint-sensitive shell tasks by adding pipefail handling where pipelines remain necessary.
- Fix backup cron allow-file management by setting explicit ownership and permissions.
